name: Deep Dark Chocolate Fudge Cookies
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces Semisweet chocolate, broken into 1/2-ounce pieces
4 ounces Unsweetened chocolate, broken into 1/2-ounce pieces
1 1/2 cups Tightly packed light brown sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Sift together the flour, cocoa, baking soda, and salt onto waxed paper. Set aside.
3. Heat 1 inch of water in the bottom half of a double boiler over medium heat.
4. Place the semisweet and unsweetened chocolate in the top half of the double boiler.
5. Tightly cover the top with film wrap and allow to heat for 12 to 15 minutes.
6. Remove from heat and stir until smooth. Keep at room temperature until needed.
7. In the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with a paddle, beat light brown sugar and butter on medium speed for 1 minute.
8. Scrape down the bowl and beat on high for an additional 30 seconds.
9. Add the eggs, one at a time, while beating on medium and stopping to scrape down the bowl after incorporating each addition.
10. Add vanilla and beat on medium for 30 seconds.
11. Add the melted chocolate and beat on low for 10 seconds.
12. Scrape down the bowl and beat an additional 30 seconds.
13. Add the sifted flour, cocoa, and baking soda and beat on low until thoroughly combined, about 20 to 30 seconds.
14. Remove the bowl from the mixer and mix thoroughly with a rubber spatula.
15. Portion the batter into cookies on two non-stick baking sheets, using 2 level tablespoons per cookie.
16. Place the trays in the preheated oven and bake for 18 to 22 minutes, rotating trays from top to bottom halfway through the baking time.
17. Allow cookies to cool on the baking sheets for 5 to 6 minutes before transferring them to a cooling rack to thoroughly cool.
18. Store cooled cookies in a sealed plastic container. Repeat until all cookies are baked. (Makes 3 to 3 1/2 dozen cookies.)
